template.windows.description: Official STS2 Windows template with version management, mod scanning, Steam depot verification, and game file downloads wired in.
template.windows.notes: Windows now provides version management, mod scan/detail/toggle flows, Steam depot verification and game file downloads, plus local launching; no runtime manager is needed.
windows.steam.0004: Comparing the local directory with the latest Steam Windows depot build.
windows.steam.0007: No version is selected, so game files cannot be downloaded.
windows.steam.0008: The current version does not have a game directory yet.
windows.steam.0009: Preparing repair download: fetching Steam depot metadata.
windows.steam.0010: Reusing the last Steam verification result to build the download plan.
windows.steam.0011: Verifying the current directory and collecting files that need repair.
windows.steam.0012: Verification finished. Building the download plan.
windows.steam.0013: The current directory is already up to date.
windows.steam.0017: Game files are now synced with the latest Steam Windows build.
windows.steam.0018: The download finished, but some files are still not aligned.
windows.steam.0019: No version is selected, so Steam verification cannot run.
windows.steam.0020: The current version does not have a game directory yet.
windows.steam.0021: Preparing Steam verification: fetching Steam depot metadata.
windows.steam.0024: CAuth did not return detailed verify entries, so the repair plan cannot be built right now.
windows.steam.0025: Reused the last Steam verification result: the current directory does not need downloads.
windows.steam.0026: Reused the last Steam verification result to build the download plan. A verification will run again after download.
windows.steam.0029: Local file scan completed. Starting the Steam verification task.
windows.steam.0032: Steam verification did not return a result.
windows.steam.0036: Failed to obtain the manifest request code. The current Steam session may be invalid, or the depot / manifest / branch pairing may be incorrect.
windows.steam.0039: Reading the manifest file list failed.
windows.steam.0040: The manifest contains more files than the current list limit can handle, so precise verification cannot continue.
windows.steam.0042: Failed to load the STS2 depot preflight result. Check network connectivity and whether the saved Steam session is still valid.
windows.steam.0043: No usable Windows depot was found. The selected account may not have access to the required content.
windows.steam.0044: The current directory matches the latest Steam Windows depot build; extra local files were ignored.
windows.steam.0045: The current directory matches the latest Steam Windows depot build.
windows.steam.0046: Missing or mismatched files were found. You can repair them with download. Extra local files do not count as a problem.
windows.steam.0047: The download was paused and can be resumed later.
windows.steam.0064: Failed to obtain the depot key: the selected Steam account may not own STS2 or the required Windows depot entitlement.
windows.steam.0065: Failed to obtain the depot key: the account session may be invalid. Please sign in to this Steam account again.
windows.steam.0066: Failed to obtain the depot key: the current depot parameters are invalid, or Steam returned invalid depot metadata.
windows.steam.0067: Failed to obtain the depot key. Make sure the selected Steam account owns the game and the session is still valid.
windows.steam.0068: Steam returned access denied: the selected account may not own STS2 or the required Windows depot entitlement.
windows.steam.0069: Steam returned an invalid session: the account session may have expired. Please sign in again.
windows.steam.0070: Steam returned invalid parameters: the current depot / manifest / branch pairing may be incorrect.
windows.steam.0071: There seems to be a network problem, so the Steam operation cannot finish right now.
windows.steam.0072: The selected account is not a Steam account.
windows.steam.0073: Choose a saved Steam account first.
windows.steam.0074: The selected account is not a Steam account.
windows.steam.0075: The selected Steam account has an invalid SteamID.
windows.steam.0076: There are no saved Steam accounts yet.
windows.steam.0077: Choose a saved Steam account for verification and download.
windows.steam.0083: Missing or mismatched files were found.
windows.steam.0085: You can directly download and repair the current directory.
windows.steam.0103: Simple verification passed: SlayTheSpire2.pck was found.
windows.steam.0104: Simple verification failed: SlayTheSpire2.pck was not found.
windows.steam.0105: No version is selected yet, so Steam verification cannot run.
windows.steam.0106: The current version does not have a game directory yet.
windows.steam.0107: Choose a saved Steam account in the Steam account section first.
windows.steam.0108: Steam verification has not completed yet. Run Steam depot verification once before launching.
windows.steam.0109: Steam verification has not completed yet. Run Steam depot verification once before launching.
windows.steam.0111: The last Steam verification did not pass.
windows.steam.0114: When disabled, this falls back to simple verification and only checks SlayTheSpire2.pck.
windows.steam.0116: Verification and download currently share the same Steam branch selection.
windows.entrypoint.0004: Pick a current version first. On Windows the mod directory automatically follows \"<game directory>\\\\mods\".
windows.entrypoint.0005: Windows now scans the current version's mods directory directly and supports viewing details, enabling or disabling, and deleting mods.
windows.entrypoint.0014: Adjust the current version's launch mode and save directory here.
windows.entrypoint.0017: The current instance {0} uses these settings to decide the save directory.
windows.entrypoint.0018: Pick a current version in the version manager before adjusting launch options.
windows.entrypoint.0021: Local launch uses default\\\\{Client ID}. Steam launch requires choosing one detected steam save directory.
windows.entrypoint.0023: Select a current version first before Windows launch options can take effect.
windows.entrypoint.0025: This only changes the save-directory rule and does not alter the game directory.
windows.entrypoint.0029: This decides the --rendering-driver argument passed to SlayTheSpire2.exe at launch.
windows.entrypoint.0031: When enabled, launch will append --fastmp=host/client. The default role is client.
windows.entrypoint.0037: When enabled, launch uses a console window and appends --log.
windows.entrypoint.0040: No steam save directories were detected yet. Launch the Steam copy once first, or refresh this page later.
windows.entrypoint.0041: Choose one detected directory. Steam launch will use it directly.
windows.entrypoint.0043: This shows the save path currently used by the launch bar.
windows.entrypoint.0045: Windows scans the current version's mods directory directly and reads enabled states from settings.save.
windows.entrypoint.0054: Pick a current version on the home page or in the version manager before the mod manager can scan a directory.
windows.entrypoint.0056: The current version does not have a game directory yet, so the mod directory cannot be derived.
windows.entrypoint.0058: The mods directory for the current version does not exist yet. Check the game directory first.
windows.entrypoint.0060: No mod manifests were found in the current mods directory yet.
windows.entrypoint.0063: This page lists all running processes for the current instance so you can force close one by Client ID.
windows.entrypoint.0066: No running processes are currently recorded for this instance.
windows.entrypoint.0069: If a Steam copy of STS2 is detected, the game directory is filled automatically when creating a version, and you can still edit it manually.
windows.entrypoint.0071: Create a new Windows version for {0}.
windows.entrypoint.0072: A Steam copy of STS2 was detected, and its game directory will be prefilled when you create a version.
windows.entrypoint.0075: This section lists every version in the current instance. Click an entry to select that version directly, and use the i button on the right to open its detail page. The selected one is highlighted.
windows.entrypoint.0077: Create a version first. If a Steam copy of STS2 is detected locally, its game directory will be prefilled for you.
windows.entrypoint.0079: Click to select this version directly.
windows.entrypoint.0082: On Windows you only need the game directory. The mod directory is derived automatically, and the default local save path starts with Client ID 1.
windows.entrypoint.0083: A Steam copy of STS2 was detected, so the game directory is prefilled first. The mod directory is derived automatically, and the default local save path starts with Client ID 1.
windows.entrypoint.0085: A new Windows version is being created for {0}.
windows.entrypoint.0088: This version is selected and can be edited directly.
windows.entrypoint.0089: Edit fields here or delete it directly.
windows.entrypoint.0096: Leave blank to use the smallest available version number. Manual values only need to stay unique within the current instance.
windows.entrypoint.0099: Can be blank. The UI falls back to the version number.
windows.entrypoint.0101: A Steam copy of STS2 was detected, so its game directory is prefilled here first, and you can still change it.
windows.entrypoint.0102: This field is required on Windows.
windows.entrypoint.0103: The mod directory always follows the mods subdirectory under this path.
windows.entrypoint.0106: The mod directory always follows the game directory. By default the local save path starts with Client ID 1, and later you can switch to Steam mode or set a separate Client ID in Launch Options.
windows.entrypoint.0111: Client ID must be an integer greater than or equal to 1. Leave it blank to use 1.
windows.entrypoint.0112: Leave it blank to use 1. Local launch uses default\\\\{Client ID}.
windows.entrypoint.0121: The current version does not have a game directory yet, so the mod directory cannot be derived.
windows.entrypoint.0122: The mods directory for the current version does not exist yet.
windows.entrypoint.0123: Scan finished, but some mods or manifests need attention.
windows.entrypoint.0128: The manifest declares a DLL, but the matching DLL file was not found
windows.entrypoint.0129: The manifest declares a PCK, but the matching PCK file was not found
windows.entrypoint.0130: The manifest does not declare a DLL or PCK
windows.entrypoint.0136: settings.save could not be read, so this mod's enabled state cannot be changed right now.
windows.entrypoint.0137: The current version does not have an available save directory yet, so the mod state cannot be written.
windows.entrypoint.0138: The global mod switch in settings.save is currently off. Changing this will also turn the global mod switch back on.
windows.entrypoint.0139: This toggle writes directly to the matching mod state in settings.save.
windows.entrypoint.0140: This mod does not have an entry in settings.save yet, so it is currently treated as enabled by default. Toggling it writes an explicit state.
windows.entrypoint.0145: Read failed (shown as enabled by default)
windows.entrypoint.0146: Disabled (the global mod switch in settings.save is off)
windows.entrypoint.0159: No save directory is available yet.
windows.steam.download.resuming_paused_t: Resuming the previously paused download task.
windows.steam.download.preparing_repair_: Preparing the list of game files that need repair.
windows.steam.host_service_unavailable: The launcher did not provide the Steam Depot service, so Steam verification or download cannot run.
